---
title: "Week 8 — Multiple / logistic regression interpretation"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

What changes when we account for other variables?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Multiple regression as adjustment; what "holding constant" actually
does, and a short look at yes/no outcomes (logistic interpretation).

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Compare an unadjusted and an adjusted coefficient from a regression
output and explain the change.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new scenario; short
adjusted-model interpretation prompt.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 8 (model-mlr) and Ch 25 (inf-model-mlr); light
cherry-picked items from Ch 9 / Ch 26 for yes/no outcome framing.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

ISLBS biomedical adjusted-OR examples once mirrored; custom small
adjusted-vs-unadjusted cases.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Interpret an adjusted coefficient.
- [ ] Explain why adjustment can change a conclusion.
- [ ] Recognize the role of regression in confounding control.
- [ ] Read simple yes/no outcome model language without technical
      derivation.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
